name: product-mvp
description: Platform-neutral UI surface for the Houra Product MVP client.

implementation_boundary.canonical_behavior_source: contracts, test-vectors, and this UI surface definition
implementation_notes: Adapters may choose native layout, navigation, and component libraries.
Adapters must preserve screen semantics, action availability, pending states, and acceptance flow coverage.
This file does not define server behavior, SDK API shape, storage layout, or framework-specific component structure.

screens.purpose: Create or resume a user session against a selected Houra server.
Run optional Product MVP vNext account recovery, email verification, and IdP login actions only when SPEC-070 capabilities are advertised.
Show joined rooms and create a room for the active session.
Show the selected room timeline and send a text message.
Upload media metadata, verify same-origin content download, and surface optional vNext transfer or encrypted attachment actions only when advertised.
Show session-owned server configuration and end the session.

actions.success_expectation: Session is stored by the host adapter and room data is loaded.
actions.error_expectation: Authentication errors are visible without losing user input.

actions.success_expectation: Registered session is stored by the host adapter and room data is loaded.
actions.error_expectation: Registration errors are visible without losing user input.

actions.success_expectation: Account recovery actions are visible only when the selected server advertises SPEC-070 capabilities.
actions.error_expectation: Unsupported recovery remains hidden or disabled without probing unadvertised endpoints.

actions.success_expectation: A redacted recovery state is available and the user is directed to check the recovery channel.
actions.error_expectation: Password reset request errors are visible without revealing account existence.

actions.success_expectation: Password reset completes and the user is returned to password login.
actions.error_expectation: Invalid or expired recovery token errors are visible without clearing entered email.

actions.success_expectation: A redacted email verification state is available for the authenticated account.
actions.error_expectation: Email verification request errors are visible without logging token or provider details.

actions.success_expectation: The authenticated account records email ownership proof.
actions.error_expectation: Invalid or expired verification token errors are visible without clearing the session.

actions.success_expectation: The host receives a redirect descriptor and owns browser presentation.
actions.error_expectation: IdP start errors are visible without launching a browser to an untrusted URL.

actions.success_expectation: The host receives a session response with the same storage boundary as password login.
actions.error_expectation: IdP completion errors are visible without logging callback query values.

actions.success_expectation: Created room becomes selectable and its timeline can load.
actions.error_expectation: Room creation errors are visible and the prior room selection is preserved.

actions.success_expectation: Selected room timeline is loaded.
actions.error_expectation: Timeline loading errors are visible and room list remains available.

actions.success_expectation: Message composer clears and the new message appears in the timeline.
actions.error_expectation: Send errors are visible and the unsent message remains editable.

actions.success_expectation: Media metadata and downloaded content summary are visible.
actions.error_expectation: Media errors are visible and input fields remain editable.

actions.success_expectation: Thumbnail metadata and preview summary are visible without exposing storage paths or raw bytes.
actions.error_expectation: Thumbnail errors are visible and media metadata remains available.

actions.success_expectation: Partial content progress is visible and tied to the advertised validator.
actions.error_expectation: Range errors are visible without deleting host-owned partial download state.

actions.success_expectation: A resumable download continues only when the advertised validator still matches host-owned resume state.
actions.error_expectation: Stale resume state is visible and restart remains available without exposing local paths.

actions.success_expectation: Encrypted attachment metadata is visible only after SPEC-072 capability metadata is advertised and validated.
actions.error_expectation: Malformed or unsupported encrypted media remains hidden or disabled without probing unadvertised endpoints.

actions.success_expectation: Ciphertext download completes as an opaque host-owned handoff without exposing local paths or plaintext bytes.
actions.error_expectation: Download errors are recoverable and keep retry controls visible without logging signed URLs or tokens.

actions.success_expectation: The crypto adapter returns a decrypted summary or preview descriptor without SDK core owning plaintext lifecycle.
actions.error_expectation: Missing-key or wrong-key states remain visible, trust copy stays bounded, and plaintext preview/share/export stay disabled.

actions.success_expectation: A recoverable encrypted media failure retries only the failed download or decrypt handoff step.
actions.error_expectation: Repeated recoverable failures stay bounded and do not clear host-owned ciphertext or retry state without host policy.

actions.success_expectation: Room list and selected timeline reflect server state.
actions.error_expectation: Refresh errors are visible without clearing the session.

actions.success_expectation: Host adapter clears the session and returns to the unauthenticated surface.
actions.error_expectation: Logout errors are visible; host adapter may still clear local session only after explicit implementation policy.

states.description: No stored host-owned session is active.
A host-owned access token and user id are available.
An async action is in flight; duplicate action submission is disabled.
A login or account registration request is in flight.
An account recovery or email verification request is in flight.
An identity provider login start or completion request is in flight.
A room creation request is in flight.
A timeline loading request is in flight.
A message send request is in flight.
A media roundtrip request is in flight.
A thumbnail, ranged download, or resumable download request is in flight.
An encrypted attachment metadata, download, decrypt, or retry action is in flight.
A refresh request is in flight.
A logout request is in flight.
No room is selected for timeline or message actions.
The message composer has no sendable text.
A room id is selected for timeline and message actions.
Latest media metadata and content download summary are available.
Latest thumbnail descriptor and preview summary are available without raw bytes.
Visible download progress is derived from public content length and range metadata.
Host-owned resumable download state is available without local path values.
Validated encrypted attachment metadata is available without raw media keys.
A host-owned ciphertext or decrypt result descriptor is available without plaintext bytes.
The encrypted attachment has been redacted and cannot be downloaded or decrypted.
The selected media object has not advertised SPEC-071 transfer capabilities.
The selected media object has not advertised SPEC-072 encrypted attachment capabilities.
Encrypted attachment metadata is present but the host or crypto adapter has no usable media key.
Redacted account recovery state is available without token values.
A host-owned identity provider redirect descriptor is available.
The selected server has not advertised SPEC-070 account recovery or IdP login capabilities.
The most recent recoverable error is visible to the user.
An encrypted media download or decrypt handoff failed in a recoverable way.

acceptance_flows.description: Manual UI flow that proves Product MVP behavior against a live Houra server; Docker Compose startup evidence is tracked separately as deploy smoke.

steps.expectation: A new localpart and password create an authenticated session.
A room is created and appears in the room list.
A text message appears in the selected room timeline.
Media metadata and content download summary are visible.
Room and timeline state reload from the server.
The session is cleared and the unauthenticated surface is shown.
The same localpart and password resume an authenticated session.

acceptance_flows.description: Optional Product MVP vNext UI flow for SPEC-071 thumbnails, ranged download, and resumable download; not part of the current Product MVP happy path until evidence includes it.

steps.expectation: Thumbnail, range, and resume actions are visible only when media metadata advertises SPEC-071 transfer capabilities.
Thumbnail preview summary is visible without recording raw bytes or local cache paths.
Partial content progress is visible and associated with the advertised validator.
Resume continues only with a matching validator; stale state is surfaced as recoverable.

acceptance_flows.description: Optional Product MVP vNext UI flow for SPEC-072 encrypted attachment metadata, ciphertext download, decrypt handoff, and recoverable errors; not part of the current Product MVP happy path until evidence includes it.

steps.expectation: Encrypted attachment actions are visible only when media metadata advertises SPEC-072 encrypted_attachment capabilities.
Validated metadata is visible without raw media keys, local paths, signed URLs, or plaintext bytes.
Ciphertext download is treated as opaque and handed to host-owned storage without plaintext preview.
Decrypt handoff goes through the crypto adapter and missing-key or wrong-key states do not render plaintext.
Recoverable errors keep retry controls visible while duplicate retry submission is disabled.

acceptance_flows.description: Optional Product MVP vNext UI flow for SPEC-070 account recovery and IdP login; not part of the current Product MVP happy path until evidence includes it.

steps.expectation: Recovery actions are available only when the server advertises SPEC-070 capabilities.
The UI records redacted recovery state and does not reveal account existence.
Password reset completes and returns the user to login rather than silently creating a session.
The host receives a redirect descriptor and owns browser presentation.
The host receives a session response and keeps token storage outside UI-free core.
An authenticated account receives redacted verification state.
The authenticated account records email ownership proof.

required_checks: record houra-spec ref and design/ui-surfaces/product-mvp.json ref
record consumer repo ref and app target
map every screen id to implementation screen semantics
map every action id to availability, pending state, and disabled conditions
confirm duplicate-submit prevention for actions with disabled_when pending.any
confirm recoverable error visibility for every action error_expectation
run or manually record product-mvp-happy-path acceptance flow coverage
if SPEC-070 is included, record product-mvp-account-recovery-vnext coverage and advertised capability evidence
if SPEC-071 is included, record product-mvp-media-transfer-vnext coverage and advertised metadata capability evidence
if SPEC-072 is included, record product-mvp-encrypted-media-vnext coverage, advertised encrypted_attachment capability evidence, crypto-adapter handoff evidence, and trust copy boundaries
record accessibility pass or explicit blocker for form labels, buttons, status, and focus order
redaction_policy: do not record raw bearer tokens or refresh tokens
do not record reset tokens, email verification tokens, authorization codes, callback URL query values, or IdP session identifiers
do not record signed or credentialed URLs, local filesystem paths, plaintext media bytes, media keys, room keys, recovery keys, decrypted thumbnails, or cache filenames exposing user data
do not record database URLs or private local paths
do not record machine-specific environment values
record redacted server base URL shape only when needed
adoption_boundaries: Expo React Native, Flutter, Web, or native component hierarchy remains adapter-owned
UI-free core evidence does not satisfy manual UI acceptance by itself
Docker Compose deploy smoke is separate from UI surface adoption evidence
SPEC-070 account recovery and IdP login actions are optional vNext coverage and require advertised capability evidence
SPEC-071 thumbnail, range, and resumable download actions are optional vNext coverage and require advertised metadata capability evidence
SPEC-072 encrypted attachment actions are optional vNext coverage and require advertised metadata capability, crypto-adapter handoff, trust copy, and redaction evidence
Matrix full compliance is not claimed by this surface

limitations: This surface does not define marketing pages or onboarding copy outside the Product MVP operation flow.
This surface does not define framework-specific navigation, component hierarchy, animations, or persistence APIs.
Federation and Matrix full-spec coverage are outside this Product MVP surface.

SPEC-071 thumbnails, range requests, and resumable download are optional Product MVP vNext actions and are outside the current Product MVP happy path until a release candidate includes matching adoption evidence.
SPEC-070 account recovery and IdP login are optional Product MVP vNext actions and are outside the current Product MVP happy path until a release candidate includes matching adoption evidence.

SPEC-072 encrypted attachment metadata, ciphertext download, decrypt handoff, recoverable errors, and trust copy are optional Product MVP vNext actions and are outside the current Product MVP happy path until a release candidate includes matching adoption evidence.
